segments.text: "This is what the Lord GOD showed me: I saw a basket of summer fruit.[^30]
Amos, what do you see?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He asked.
A basket of summer fruit,
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text:
I replied.
So the LORD said to me,
The end has come for My people Israel; I will no longer spare them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: In that day,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: declares the Lord GOD,
the songs[^31] of the temple[^32] will turn to wailing. Many will be the corpses, strewn in silence everywhere!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "Hear this, you who trample the needy, who do away with the poor of the land,
"asking,
When will the New Moon be over, that we may sell grain? When will the Sabbath end, that we may market wheat? Let us reduce the ephah and increase the shekel;[^33] let us cheat with dishonest scales.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Let us buy the poor with silver and the needy for a pair of sandals,
selling even the chaff with the wheat!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "The LORD has sworn by the Pride of Jacob:
I will never forget any of their deeds.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Will not the land quake for this, and all its dwellers mourn? All of it will swell like the Nile; it will surge and then subside
like the Nile in Egypt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And in that day, declares the Lord GOD, I will make the sun go down at noon, and I will darken the earth in the daytime.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will turn your feasts into mourning and all your songs into lamentation. I will cause everyone to wear sackcloth and every head to be shaved. I will make it like a time of mourning for an only son, and its outcome like a bitter day.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord GOD,
when I will send a famine on the land—not a famine of bread or a thirst for water, but a famine of hearing the words of the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: People will stagger from sea to sea and roam from north to east, seeking the word of the LORD, but they will not find it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
In that day the lovely young women—the young men as well—
will faint from thirst.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who swear by the guilt of Samaria and say, ‘As surely as your god lives, O Dan,’or, ‘As surely as the way[^34] of Beersheba lives’—they will fall, never to rise again.
segments.speaker: Unknown
